zh.lifeSummary: 當Margery Bert於大約1530年出生在英國素福爾可斯特拉德布洛可時，她的父親William Bert35歲，母親Johanna Godbold31歲。她於1551年10月15日與William Fisher在英國英格蘭素福爾可斯特拉德布洛可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1591年8月9日在英國素福爾可希勒哈姆享年62歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可希勒哈姆。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margery Bert于大约1530年出生在英国素福爾可斯特拉德布洛可时，她的父亲William Bert35岁，母亲Johanna Godbold31岁。她于1551年10月15日与William Fisher在英国英格兰素福爾可斯特拉德布洛可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和7个女儿的父母。她去世于1591年8月9日在英国素福爾可希勒哈姆享年62岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可希勒哈姆。

nameMeanings.name: Bert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Philibert, Pierre.
German, French, and Polish: from a short form of various compound personal names formed with ancient Germanic berht ‘bright, famous’ (see for example Bertram ).
English: from the Middle English female personal name Berte (Old French Berte, from ancient Germanic Berta ).

variants.name: Berton
meanings: French: from a pet form of the personal name   Bert  .

History: Pierre Bert(h)on or Bertin, a Huguenot from the French province of Poitou, took in 1686 the lead of the short-lived Narragansett colony, in RI. His surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors (as Pierre Bertin or Bertine or Bertain or Bartain or Berton or Bretin) and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America (as Pierre Bertine).

variants.name: Delbert
meanings: French: patronymic from the personal name   Bert  .

variants.name: Bettencourt
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Jorge, Luis, Ana, Carlos, Joaquin, Aires, Arlindo, Armando, Francisco, Mario.  Portuguese Joao, Adao, Albano, Conceicao, Duarte, Ilidio, Paulo, Serafim.

French (northern) and Portuguese (of French origin; mainly Azores): habitational name from any of various places in northern France so called, with minor variations in spelling, of which the main one is in Somme. They are named with the ancient Germanic personal name Betto, an assimilated form of Berto (see Bert ) + Old French court ‘farm(yard)’. This surname is now much more frequent in Portugal than in France; to Portugal it was brought in the 15th century. See also French Bethencourt and Spanish Betancourt .

variants.name: Berteau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Leonce.
from a variant of the personal name Bertaud, from ancient Germanic Berhtwald, composed of the elements berht ‘bright’ and wald ‘power, authority’.

variants.name: Bertram
meanings: German, English (mainly in southeastern Scotland, of Norman origin), French, and Danish: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bertram, composed of the elements berht ‘bright, famous’ + hramn ‘raven’. The raven was the bird of Odin, king of the gods, in ancient Germanic mythology. The personal name was common in France throughout the Middle Ages (especially in the form Bertrand ), where its popularity was increased by the fame of the troubadour Bertrand de Born (c. 1140–1214). The name was taken to England by the Normans in the forms Bertran(d), Bertram, and Bartram.

variants.name: Bethune
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin); French (Béthune): habitational name from Béthune in Pas-de-Calais, Picardy, France, recorded in the 8th century in the Latin form Bitunia, probably an adjective (with villa understood) derived from the oblique form of an ancient Germanic personal name Betto, an assimilated form of Berto (see Bert ). See also Scottish Beaton .

variants.name: Bright
meanings: English: usually a nickname from Middle English bright ‘bright, good-looking, fresh-faced’ (Old English beorht ‘bright, shining’).

English: perhaps occasionally from an unrecorded Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Briht, Beorht, from a short form of any of several Old English personal names of which beorht was the first element, such as Beorhtrīc ‘bright’ + ‘powerful’. Compare Bert .

nameMeanings.name: Margery
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual medieval vernacular form of   Margaret   (now also commonly spelled   Marjorie  ). This form of the name is preserved in the nursery rhyme ‘See-saw, Margery Daw’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Margery Bert was born about 1530, in Stradbroke, Suffolk, England, her father, William Bert, was 35 and her mother, Johanna Godbold, was 31. She married William Fisher on 15 October 1551, in Stradbroke, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 7 daughters. She died on 9 August 1591, in Syleham, Suffolk, England, at the age of 62, and was buried in Syleham, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margery Bert est née environ 1530, à Stradbroke, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, William Bert, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Johanna Godbold, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé William Fisher le 15 octobre 1551, à Stradbroke, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée le 9 août 1591, à Syleham, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Syleham, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margery Bert um 1530 in Stradbroke, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Bert 35 und ihre Mutter Johanna Godbold 31. Sie hat am 15. Oktober 1551 in Stradbroke, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom William Fisher geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb am 9. August 1591 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Syleham, Suffolk, England und wurde in Syleham, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margery Bert è nata il circa 1530 nel Stradbroke, Suffolk, England, sua padre, William Bert, aveva 35 anni e sua madre, Johanna Godbold, aveva 31 anni. Lei sposato William Fisher il 15 ottobre 1551, nel Stradbroke, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 7 figlie. Morì il 9 agosto 1591, nel Syleham, Suffolk, England, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolta a Syleham, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Margery Bertが約1530年にイギリススフォルクストラドブロクで生まれた時，父William Bertは35歳，母Johanna Godboldは31歳であった。1551年10月15日にイギリスイギリススフォルクストラドブロクでWilliam Fisherと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼女は1591年8月9日にイギリススフォルクシレハムで62歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクシレハムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margery Bert(이)가 대략 1530년 영국 수폴크 스트라드브로크에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Bert(은)는 35세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Johanna Godbold(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 1551년 10월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 스트라드브로크에서 William Fisher(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1591년 8월 9일 영국 수폴크 실함에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 실함에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margery Bert nasceu aproximadamente 1530, em Stradbroke, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, William Bert, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Johanna Godbold, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com William Fisher em 15 de outubro de 1551, em Stradbroke, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 7 filhas. Ela faleceu em 9 de agosto de 1591, em Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margery Bert родилась около 1530 г. в местности страдброк, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William Bert было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Johanna Godbold, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Fisher 15 октября 1551 г. в местности страдброк, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 7 дочерей. Она умерла 9 августа 1591 г. в местности силэам, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности силэам, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margery Bert nació aproximadamente 1530, en Stradbroke, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, William Bert, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Johanna Godbold, tenía 31 años. Se casó con William Fisher el 15 de octubre de 1551, en Stradbroke, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió el 9 de agosto de 1591, en Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Syleham, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
